records.ety: [OE. vinegre, F. vinaigre; vin wine (L. vinum) + aigre sour. See Wine, and Eager, a.]

defs: A sour liquid used as a condiment, or as a preservative, and obtained by the spontaneous (acetous) fermentation, or by the artificial oxidation, of wine, cider, beer, or the like.
notes: ☞ The characteristic sourness of vinegar is due to acetic acid, of which it contains from three to five per cent. Wine vinegar contains also tartaric acid, citric acid, etc.

defs: Hence, anything sour; -- used also metaphorically.
quotes: Here's the challenge: . . . I warrant there's vinegar and pepper in't.

defs: To convert into vinegar; to make like vinegar; to render sour or sharp.
quotes: Hoping that he hath vinegared his senses As he was bid.
